data.title: Somnigroup International declares $0.15 dividend
data.description:
• Somnigroup International (NYSE:SGI) declares $0.15/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 0.99%

  • See SGI Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Viatris hits main goals in Phase 3 trial for birth control patch
data.description:
• Viatris (NASDAQ:VTRS) announced Thursday that Xulane Lo, a birth control patch designed to deliver a low estrogen dose for women of childbearing potential, reached primary and all secondary endpoints.
  • The Pfizer (PFE) spinoff noted that Xulane Lo indicated contraceptive efficacy as its Pearl Index, a standard used in clinical trials to calculate the efficacy of contraceptives, reached 4.14.
  • Additionally, the product was found to have a favorable safety and tolerability profile, with most treatment-emergent adverse events being mild to moderate in severity.
  • The trial evaluated the Xulane Lo low-dose weekly dermal patch in over 1,200 healthy women aged at least 16 years in 81 study sites across the U.S., Puerto Rico, and Canada.
  • The company plans to submit a new drug application seeking U.S. FDA approval for Xulane Lo in the second half of 2025.

data.title: Vital Farms GAAP EPS of $0.37 beats by $0.11, revenue of $162.2M misses by $0.78M
data.description:
• Vital Farms press release (NASDAQ:VITL): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.37 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $162.2M (+9.6% Y/Y) misses by $0.78M.
  • Gross Margin of 38.5%, compared to 39.8%
  • Net Income of $16.9 million, compared to $19.0 million
  • Net Income per Diluted Share of $0.37, compared to $0.43
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $27.5 million, compared to $29.1 million1

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: ConocoPhillips tops Q1 profit estimates, cuts adj. operating costs outlook
Jumia Technologies AG reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Jumia Technologies AG press release (NYSE:JMIA): Q1
  • Revenue of $36.3M (-25.8% Y/Y) misses by $2.94M.

  • GMV of $161.7 million, down 11% year-over-year, or down 2% in constant currency.

  • Orders grew 21% year-over-year, the highest growth rate in the past two years.

  • Quarterly Active Customers ordering physical goods grew by 15% year-over-year.

  • FY25 Guidance

    • We anticipate physical goods Orders to grow between 20% and 25% year-over-year, up from the previous range of 15-20%.

    • GMV is projected to be between $795 million and $830 million in 2025, a year-over-year increase of 10% and 15%, respectively, excluding foreign exchange impacts.

    • We forecast Loss before Income Tax to be in the range of negative $50 million to negative $55 million, a year-over-year decrease of 49% and 44%, respectively.

    • For full-year 2026:

      • We are projecting Loss before income tax to be in the range of negative $25-$30 million

      • We believe to be on track to achieve breakeven on a Loss before income tax basis in the fourth quarter of 2026, and deliver full-year profitability in 2027

data.title: Allegro MicroSystems Non-GAAP EPS of $0.06 beats by $0.01, revenue of $192.8M beats by $7.44M
data.description:
• Allegro MicroSystems press release (NASDAQ:ALGM): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.06 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $192.8M (+15.5% Y/Y) beats by $7.44M.

  • The Company also estimates the following results on a non-GAAP basis:

    • Gross Margin is expected to be between 46% and 48%,
    • Interest expense of approximately $5 million inclusive of a $25 million voluntary debt payment made on April 30, and
    • Diluted Earnings per Share are expected to be between $0.06 and $0.10.

data.title: Insmed reports mixed Q1 results; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Insmed press release (NASDAQ:INSM): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$1.42 misses by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $92.82M (+22.9% Y/Y) beats by $0.73M.
  • As of March 31, 2025, Insmed had cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaling approximately $1.2 billion.
  • Insmed continues to anticipate full-year 2025 global ARIKAYCE revenues in the range of $405 million to $425 million, representing between 11% and 17% year-over-year growth compared to 2024.
  • The Company plans to continue to invest in the following key activities in 2025:

    • (i)  commercialization and expansion of ARIKAYCE globally;

    (ii)  commercial launch of brensocatib in the U.S., if approved, with advancement of regulatory submissions and interactions in the EU, UK, and Japan;

    (iii)  advancement of clinical trial programs for brensocatib, including the ongoing Phase 2b BiRCh study in patients with CRSsNP and the Phase 2b CEDAR study in patients with HS;

    (iv)  advancement of the Phase 3 ENCORE study for ARIKAYCE, which is intended to satisfy the post-marketing requirement for full approval of its current indication and potentially support label expansion to include all patients with a MAC lung disease;

    (v)  advancement of the clinical development programs for TPIP, including the Phase 2b study in patients with PAH and the initiation of a Phase 3 study in patients with PH-ILD;

    (vi)  advancement of the Phase 1 ASCEND study for INS1201 in DMD; and

    (vii)  continued development of its pre-clinical research programs.

data.title: AxoGen Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.02 misses by $0.04, revenue of $48.56M beats by $0.21M
data.description:
• AxoGen press release (NASDAQ:AXGN): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.02 misses by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $48.56M (+17.3% Y/Y) beats by $0.21M.
  • We are maintaining our full year revenue growth guidance in the range of 15% to 17% and continue to expect gross margin to be in the range of 73% to 75%. This includes one-time costs, mainly related to the anticipated BLA approval, impacting gross margin by approximately 1%. Additionally, we reiterate that we expect to be net cash flow positive for the full year.

data.title: Anheuser-Busch InBev gains after strong performances from Michelob Ultra, Cutwater, and Nütrl
data.description:
Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) gained in early trading on Thursday after reporting a more profitable quarter than anticipated.

Revenue increased by 1.5% in Q1 on a currency neutral basis, with revenue per hl growth of 3.7%. The Megabrands segment saw 4.4% revenue growth, led by Corona. Michelob Ultra drove market share gains in the U.S.

Normalized EBITDA increased by 7.9%, with margin expansion of 218 basis points driven by top-line growth, cost of sales tailwinds and disciplined overhead management. Underlying Profit was reported at $1.61 billion to smash the consensus expectation and top the  $1.51 tally from a year ago.

The Beyond Beer segment saw sales accelerate 16.6% during the quarter, led by double-digit growth of Cutwater and Nütrl in the U.S. and Beats in Brazil.

"The strength of the beer category and the continued momentum of our megabrands delivered another quarter of profitable growth. EBITDA increased at the top-end of our outlook and the ongoing optimization of our business drove Underlying EPS growth of 7.1%. The consistent execution of our strategy by our teams and partners drove a solid start to the year and reinforces our confidence in delivering on our outlook for 2025," stated CEO Michel Doukeris, CEO,

Looking ahead, Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) expects EBITDA to grow in line with the medium-term outlook of between 4% to 8%. The outlook for FY25 reflects the company's current assessment of inflation and other macroeconomic conditions.

Shares of Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) rose 1.9% in premarket trading to contrast to the 8.6% slide for Molson Coors (TAP), which reported weak results for a roughly overlapping quarter.

data.title: Papa John's Non-GAAP EPS of $0.36 beats by $0.01, revenue of $518.3M beats by $4.33M
data.description:
• Papa John's press release (NASDAQ:PZZA): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.36 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $518.3M (+0.9% Y/Y) beats by $4.33M.
  • North America comparable sales were down 3% from a year ago as Domestic Company-owned restaurants were down 5% and North America franchised restaurants were down 2%; International comparable sales were up 3% compared with the prior year quarter.
  • Opened 47 new restaurants systemwide, comprised of 18 restaurant openings in North America and 29 restaurant openings in International markets.
  • Global system-wide restaurant sales were $1.22 billion, a 1% increase compared with the prior year first quarter, as higher International comparable sales and trailing twelve-month net restaurant growth were partially offset by lower North American comparable sales.
  • Total revenues of $518 million were up 1% compared with the prior year quarter as higher Commissary and Advertising Funds revenues were partially offset by lower revenues at our Company-owned restaurants.

  • 2025 Outlook

    The Company is reiterating its 2025 annual guidance for the following metrics:

    • System-wide sales: up 2% to 5%
    • North America comparable sales: Flat to up 2%
    • International comparable sales: Flat to up 2%
    • Restaurant development:
      • North America: 85 to 115 gross openings
      • International: 180 to 200 gross openings
    • Adjusted EBITDA (as defined below): $200 million to $220 million
    • Depreciation & amortization: $70 million to $75 million
    • Interest expense: $40 million to $45 million
    • Tax rate: 28% to 32%
    • Capital expenditures: $75 million to $85 million
